title: Tool-call responses: unique IDs across turns + OpenAI content:null
description: Two OpenAI-API-compliance bugs that manifest as 'second tool call not executed / no approval dialog' in egg-mono: (1) tool_call_id is generated as f'call_{self._tool_index}' where _tool_index resets to 0 on every request. So in a 2-turn conversation, BOTH the first and second assistant messages emit a tool_call with id 'call_0'. Clients that dedupe by id treat the second as already-seen, skip approval, don't execute. Fix: use uuid-based ids ('call_' + uuid4 hex prefix) so every tool_call is globally unique. (2) Initial streaming chunk sends {role:'assistant', content:''} -- empty string, not null. OpenAI sends content:null for tool-call responses (the content field is absent OR null in deltas that carry tool_calls). Some clients treat '' vs null differently and mis-mode. Fix: omit the content field from the initial role chunk; only include it when a CONTENT_DELTA actually arrives. Apply to all format handlers for consistency.
close_reason: both fixes landed: (1) tool_call_id is now f'call_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:24]}' in every format handler; START stores the id on the parser state (self._current_tool_call_id) and COMMIT reuses it, so each call has a globally unique id and START/COMMIT agree. (2) Initial streaming chunk emits 'content': None (JSON null), matching OpenAI's streaming convention -- previously emitted 'content': '' which pushed some strict clients (egg-mono) into content-mode and confused their tool_calls detection. Two regression tests pin the new contracts.

title: Tool_call sometimes not executed: streaming emits nothing on malformed body
description: After the Hermes streaming-args change (commit dff2a27), when the <tool_call> body is malformed in a way our JSON state-machine can't parse (the common Hermes doubled-brace tokenization quirk '{{...}}' is the smoking gun), the extractor sets errored=True and stops emitting. Only TOOL_CALL_START goes to the wire, carrying name='' and arguments=''. TOOL_CALL_COMMIT fires with the correctly-parsed name and args (via _parse_tool_call_body tolerant fallback) but openai.py's _event_to_openai_chunks does NOT project COMMIT onto the stream. Client receives finish_reason=tool_calls but no callable name, so it silently drops the tool call. Two fixes: (1) handle the doubled-brace quirk inline in the extractor so streaming succeeds; (2) track _name_emitted and _args_streamed in HermesParserState so at commit time, if the streaming extractor never got its name/args out (errored case), emit TOOL_CALL_NAME + TOOL_ARGS_DELTA(full_args) from the tolerant parse as a safety net before COMMIT.
notes: Related follow-ups landed in workspace-TBD: unique tool_call_id across turns + OpenAI content:null conformance.
close_reason: two fixes: (1) _StreamingBodyExtractor now transparently consumes the Hermes doubled-brace quirk ('{{...}}') so the common malformation streams cleanly. (2) HermesParserState tracks _current_name_emitted/_current_args_streamed flags; if the streaming extractor never got NAME or ARGS onto the wire, _commit_streaming_tool_call emits catch-up events built from _parse_tool_call_body's tolerant parse BEFORE TOOL_CALL_COMMIT, so clients always see a callable tool_call before finish_reason=tool_calls. Two new regression tests pin both fixes.

title: E2E testing with tinygrad backend
description: Create concrete end-to-end tests that load a real GGUF model via tinygrad, send tool-calling requests through the full stack (API -> Orchestrator -> Backend -> Parser -> SSE), and verify correct streaming tool call responses. This validates the entire Phase 1 pipeline works against a real model. Include: 1) Download a small GGUF model (e.g. Qwen 0.6B). 2) Test tool-calling request via OpenAI client. 3) Test content-only request. 4) Test streaming SSE format correctness. 5) Test non-streaming JSON response.
close_reason: All 12 E2E tests passing: 9 scripted (content, tool calling, SSE format, aux endpoints) + 3 real model (tinygrad + Qwen2.5-0.5B Q4_0 GGUF). Fixed tinygrad backend to use tinygrad.apps.llm API (from_gguf, SimpleTokenizer.from_gguf_kv). Switched to Q4_0 model (tinygrad lacks Q5_1 support). Fixed SQLite threading with CACHELEVEL=0.

title: Packed-quant kernels: Q4_K, Q4_0, Q6_K, Q8_0
description: Write per-quant-type unpack kernels and wire into the fused-matmul infra. These four quant types cover ~95% of GGUFs shipped today (Q4_K is the modern default). Each needs a block-layout unpack routine expressed in tinygrad tensor ops so the scheduler fuses it into the matmul kernel.

title: Sampler: temperature, top_p, top_k
description: tinygrad forward returns argmax only (temperature=0). Add logits processor pipeline between forward and sample. Start with temperature, top_p, top_k which SamplingParams already exposes. Pure Python tensor ops on the logits tensor. Required for any non-deterministic generation.
close_reason: implemented: egg_toolbox/sampling.py exposes sample_next_token(logits_np, params, rng) with temperature, top_k, top_p. numpy-based; per-request rng seeded from SamplingParams.seed so concurrent requests do not share entropy. TinygradBackend._do_load_model patches the model instance: replaces forward and forward_jit with versions that return raw logits (shape (1, vocab_size)) instead of tinygrad upstream argmax. Decode loop calls sample_next_token on logits.tolist() per step. Added numpy to project deps. 10 new sampler tests + updated 3 existing backend fakes to speak the new logits protocol.

title: Packed-quant: fused matmul infrastructure
description: Keep GGUF weights on device as packed blocks (the --keep-packed path exists but is slow because dequant runs per matmul unfused) and express the matmul as a tinygrad op graph the scheduler can fuse with the dequant. Cuts Qwen3-8B Q4 runtime memory from ~16 GB to ~5 GB. Supersedes workspace-30s at completion. Foundation for every quant-kernel bead.

title: Port: Gemma family (Gemma 2/3/4)
description: Gemma architecture differs from llama: local-global attention alternation (sliding-window on some layers), soft logit capping via tanh, tied input/output embeddings, specific norm positions (pre+post). Port the forward pass into egg_toolbox/models/gemma.py. Verify against a small Gemma 2 checkpoint end-to-end before attempting Gemma 3/4.

title: Port: DeepSeek V2/V3 (MLA attention)
description: Multi-head latent attention (MLA) compresses K/V into a latent space with absorbed projection matrices; standard QKV code does not apply. Port DeepSeek attention plus feed-forward into egg_toolbox/models/deepseek.py. Verify against DeepSeek V2 Lite 16B (smallest checkpoint). V3 is the same architecture at larger scale plus MoE, so lands alongside the MoE bead.

title: Infra: egg_toolbox.models package + base architecture class
description: Establish the package that holds per-family architecture implementations. Base class with forward(tokens, start_pos) signature matching the existing prefix-cache path. Conventions for block indexing, KV cache shape, RoPE params, tokenizer wiring, bias fixups. Unblocks every architecture port. Should let a new arch be added in one file (~300-800 lines) without touching the orchestrator or backend.

title: Epic: tinygrad -> llama.cpp-level model coverage
description: Umbrella for the work that turns egg-toolbox into tinygrads missing runtime layer. Target scope: Gemma family, DeepSeek V3, MoE variants, Phi family; fused packed-quant matmul; real sampler; GBNF grammar runtime; architecture-aware GGUF dispatch; model compatibility test matrix. When complete, a tinygrad user can load any popular open-weight GGUF with quality equivalent to llama.cpp server. Reframes egg-toolbox from universal middleware to tinygrad-native runtime.
notes: Design rationale: egg-toolbox is tinygrads missing runtime layer, not a cross-backend frontend. The bet is that tinygrads kernel scheduler replaces llama.cpps ~500k LoC of hand-tuned per-backend kernels, so bringing egg-toolbox to parity only needs architecture descriptions (~5-10k LoC for the top 20 models) plus sampler + grammar + packed-quant matmul. Known weak points to watch: MoE routing efficiency, quantized KV, long-context attention. Known strong points: dense transformers, standard attention.

title: Stream tool_call arguments incrementally for Hermes
description: Hermes parser currently buffers the entire <tool_call> body before emitting TOOL_ARGS_DELTA (see _process_in_tool + _commit_buffered_tool_call in formats/hermes.py). Rationale was that streaming raw token chunks polluted the OpenAI arguments field with the name field and JSON structural noise. Fix: write a streaming JSON state-machine that projects only the name value and the arguments value from the body, emitting TOOL_CALL_NAME once the name is known and TOOL_ARGS_DELTA character-by-character as the arguments value arrives. Handles nested object args (common), string args (rare), and JSON-literal args. Falls back to existing _parse_tool_call_body buffered parse on malformed JSON (doubled braces, etc.) so robustness is preserved. Adds the first test_hermes.py (currently missing!) with fragment-level fed tests. Affects only Hermes for now; other format handlers already use similar buffer-then-emit patterns and can be migrated in follow-ups.
close_reason: implemented: _StreamingBodyExtractor JSON state machine projects the name value and arguments value out of a Hermes tool_call body; HermesParserState emits TOOL_CALL_NAME as soon as the name closes and one TOOL_ARGS_DELTA per token that contributed args chars. Falls back to the existing _parse_tool_call_body when streaming errors out (doubled braces, etc.), preserving robustness. 9 new test_hermes.py tests cover streaming, no-leakage of name into args, args-first ordering, string/nested/escaped args, malformed fallback.

title: Prefix KV caching in TinygradBackend
description: Within a single process, reuse tinygrad's per-block cache_kv tensor across sequential generate_tokens calls by tracking the resident token sequence and computing the longest common prefix with the new prompt. Only prefill the diverging suffix at start_pos=common_len. Upstream tinygrad has the machinery (TransformerBlock.cache_kv is persistent) but Transformer.generate always overrides start_pos=0 and resets the T=1 JIT, so no prefix reuse happens. We bypass generate() and call self._model(t, start_pos) directly. Includes unit tests (fake model recording (T, start_pos) across two requests), invalidation-on-exception, and a real-model integration test using the Qwen2.5-0.5B fixture. Scope is single-tenant only: next request's common prefix is computed against the last request's tokens. Multi-tenant radix-tree caching is a separate future project.
close_reason: implemented: TinygradBackend tracks resident token sequence in _cache_tokens, bypasses Transformer.generate (which hardcodes start_pos=0), drives forward directly with the correct start_pos so cache_kv is reused for the longest common token prefix with the previous request. 6 unit tests (fake-model (T,start_pos) recorder) + 3 updated thread-pinning tests pin the contract. EGG_PREFIX_CACHE=0 disables. Single-tenant only; multi-tenant deferred.

title: Rename omnitool package to egg-toolbox (Python module: egg_toolbox)
description: Rename the Python package and CLI from 'omnitool' to 'egg-toolbox'. Python identifiers use underscore: module 'egg_toolbox', PyPI name 'egg-toolbox'. Scope: pyproject.toml, package directory, all imports, __main__ CLI, tests, context.md/README.md/docs/plan references, CLAUDE.md, venv reinstall. Repo top-level directory stays 'omnitool/' for now (user can mv later).
close_reason: Rename complete. Package egg_toolbox (was omnitool), PyPI name egg-toolbox, CLI 'python -m egg_toolbox' or 'egg-toolbox <model>'. Project dir egg-toolbox/ (was omnitool/omnitool/). All 23 tests pass. Smoke-tested server: owned_by='egg-toolbox', system_fingerprint='egg-toolbox-v0'. Added 'egg-toolbox' provider to egg-mono/eggconfig/eggconfig/data/models.json with Qwen2.5-0.5B model; verified stream_chat round-trip succeeds. Docs renamed docs/omnitool-*.md -> docs/egg-toolbox-*.md with bulk sed replace. Only remaining 'omnitool' references are historical in .beads/issues.jsonl.

title: Hermes parser: extract name field, stream arguments-only, set finish_reason=tool_calls
description:
When a Hermes <tool_call>{...}</tool_call> is emitted, the current parser:
1. Leaves tool_calls[0].function.name as empty string instead of parsing it from the JSON body
2. Streams the entire raw JSON (including {{, name, etc.) as arguments deltas — should only stream the arguments object value
3. Returns finish_reason=stop instead of tool_calls on completion

Observed with Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct + curl tool-call test (2026-04-18). Model correctly outputs <tool_call>{"name": "get_weather", "arguments": {"city": "Zagreb"}}</tool_call> but parser fails to split into name/arguments and misclassifies finish reason. Blocks tool-call round-trip (workspace-5eg).
close_reason: Fixed via egg-toolbox/egg_toolbox/formats/hermes.py refactor: new _parse_tool_call_body helper does strict JSON -> double-brace repair -> trailing-comma repair -> regex fallback. _process_in_tool now buffers full JSON body instead of streaming raw token chunks (which polluted 'arguments' with 'name' field text). _commit_buffered_tool_call always advances _tool_index, so finish_reason=tool_calls fires even for malformed bodies that opened <tool_call>. Verified with Qwen2.5-0.5B + real curl: name='get_weather', arguments='{"city":"Zagreb"}', finish_reason='tool_calls' in both streaming and non-streaming. Verified end-to-end via egg-mono stream_chat() with tools param — received clean tool_calls object. 23/23 unit tests still pass. The {{ quirk (workspace-ome) is from Qwen's tokenizer occasionally merging { with preceding whitespace into a single BPE token that decodes as {{; parser handles it robustly now.

title: First-light: tool-call round-trip via egg-mono + omnitool + Hermes model
description: Send a prompt with a simple tool (e.g., get_weather) via egg-mono. Verify omnitool's HermesParser correctly extracts <tool_call>...</tool_call>, streams tool_calls in OpenAI SSE format, and egg-mono reassembles it. Validates the full universal-IR path end-to-end.
close_reason: Fixed via egg-toolbox/egg_toolbox/formats/hermes.py refactor: new _parse_tool_call_body helper does strict JSON -> double-brace repair -> trailing-comma repair -> regex fallback. _process_in_tool now buffers full JSON body instead of streaming raw token chunks (which polluted 'arguments' with 'name' field text). _commit_buffered_tool_call always advances _tool_index, so finish_reason=tool_calls fires even for malformed bodies that opened <tool_call>. Verified with Qwen2.5-0.5B + real curl: name='get_weather', arguments='{"city":"Zagreb"}', finish_reason='tool_calls' in both streaming and non-streaming. Verified end-to-end via egg-mono stream_chat() with tools param — received clean tool_calls object. 23/23 unit tests still pass. The {{ quirk (workspace-ome) is from Qwen's tokenizer occasionally merging { with preceding whitespace into a single BPE token that decodes as {{; parser handles it robustly now.

title: First-light: wire omnitool into egg-mono models.json and round-trip one prompt
description: Add an 'omnitool' provider entry to eggllm models.json.example pointing at http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions. Run a plain chat prompt from egg-mono through omnitool. Confirm response matches what curl produced.
close_reason: Round-trip works: egg-mono LLMClient(models_path=/tmp/omnitool_egg_models.json) -> omnitool HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:8765 -> tinygrad -> Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-Q4_0. Streamed output: 'Fruit: Apple\nVegetable: Carrot'. Config template: provider 'omnitool' with api_base=http://HOST:PORT/v1/chat/completions, model_name matching the GGUF stem. No changes to egg-mono needed — OpenAICompatAdapter worked as-is.

title: First-light: smoke-test omnitool server via curl
description: Run python -m omnitool <model.gguf> --backend tinygrad and send a plain (no-tools) /v1/chat/completions request via curl. Verify streaming SSE response is well-formed OpenAI-compat. Fix any orchestrator/API issues found.
close_reason: Server starts cleanly with python -m omnitool <qwen>.gguf --backend tinygrad. Verified: /v1/models returns loaded model, /v1/chat/completions streaming and non-streaming both produce correct answers ('2 plus 2 equals 4.', 'The capital of France is Paris.'). SSE is well-formed OpenAI-compat with proper role, content deltas, finish_reason, and [DONE] terminator. Tool-call parsing has bugs filed as workspace-gb4 (name extraction / finish_reason) and workspace-ome (literal {{ artifact). Plain chat smoke-test objective is met.

title: First-light: verify tinygrad loads & generates from chosen GGUF standalone
description: Outside omnitool, write a ~20-line script using tinygrad.apps.llm.Transformer.from_gguf to load the chosen GGUF and generate a few tokens from a simple prompt. Confirms tinygrad supports the architecture/quantization before wiring through omnitool. Fix any tinygrad backend gaps discovered.
close_reason: Confirmed tinygrad loads Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-Q4_0 and generates coherent text after fixing attn_q/k/v bias loading. Patch lives in omnitool/backends/tinygrad.py::_from_gguf_with_qkv_bias. All 23 e2e tests still pass. Example output: 'I am a computer program designed to assist users in generating text and answering questions.'

title: First-light: pick & download tiny Hermes GGUF for e2e test
description: Choose a small (<1GB) GGUF model that uses Hermes tool-call format (<tool_call>) and can be loaded by tinygrad's Transformer.from_gguf. Candidates: Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct Q4_K_M, Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct Q4_K_M, or a Hermes-2-Pro small variant. Download to a known local path. Record choice + hash for reproducibility.
close_reason: Model already downloaded at omnitool/tests/fixtures/models/qwen2.5-0.5b-instruct-q4_0.gguf (409MB Q4_0) and q4_k_m.gguf (468MB). Canonical per conftest.py: Q4_0 from Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-GGUF. Qwen2.5 chat template uses Hermes <tool_call> format.

title: E2E tests for production API features
description: Tests for error handling, token counts, tool_choice, stream_options, system_fingerprint
close_reason: Closed

title: API production hardening: error handling, tool_choice, stream_options, usage, fingerprint
description: Move body parsing to middleware with error handling, support tool_choice and stream_options params, real token usage, system_fingerprint, fix /v1/models
close_reason: Closed

title: Orchestrator token tracking in DONE events
description: Enrich DONE events with actual prompt/completion token counts in both step and constraint backends
close_reason: Closed

title: Add token count fields to SemanticEvent
description: Add prompt_tokens and completion_tokens optional fields to SemanticEvent dataclass
close_reason: Closed

title: Model compatibility test matrix
description: CI job that downloads 10-15 small representative GGUFs (Qwen3 0.5B, Gemma 2B, DeepSeek 1.3B, Phi 3 Mini, Mixtral 8x1B, OLMoE 1B-7B, etc.), runs a canned tool-call scenario against each, and asserts: model loads, generates tokens, emits a tool_call parseable by the right format handler, finish_reason=tool_calls. Without this matrix, day-1 support for Model X is hope not verification.

title: Architecture-aware GGUF dispatch
description: Read general.architecture from GGUF metadata and dispatch to the right class in egg_toolbox.models. Fallback to the current llama-family loader for unknown architectures. Small ~50-line piece of code, but essential for the user-facing story: load any GGUF and it works.

title: GBNF grammar runtime (logits masking + lazy activation)
description: egg_toolbox/grammar.py already PRODUCES GBNF (workspace-2mu closed). This bead CONSUMES it at decode time: parse GBNF into a state-machine, track current position, compute allowed-next-token mask per step, apply to logits before sampling. Lazy activation: only enforce the grammar after a trigger word (e.g. <tool_call> opens) so content generation stays free-form. Turns JSON-mode (workspace-jcg) and tool-call grammars from hints into hard constraints.

title: Sampler: repetition penalty, frequency, presence
description: Completion quality fixers that penalize already-seen tokens. OpenAI clients send frequency_penalty and presence_penalty routinely. SamplingParams already has the fields; wire them into the logits pipeline. Track a running counter of emitted tokens for the penalty logic.

title: Port: MoE variants (Mixtral, Qwen-MoE, DeepSeek-MoE)
description: tinygrad already has ExpertWeights primitive (used by OLMoE). Verify behaviour matches Mixtral and Qwen-MoE routing (top-k gating, softmax weighting, shared experts in DeepSeek variant); ensure tinygrads scheduler produces efficient sparse kernels for expert selection. Target models: Mixtral 8x7B (reference), Qwen3-30B-A3B (modern).

title: Port: Phi family (Phi 3, Phi 4)
description: Phi uses partial-RoPE (rotary applied to only a fraction of head_dim), custom norm positioning, sometimes merged MLP gate+up. Port forward pass into egg_toolbox/models/phi.py. Start with Phi 3 Mini (3.8B) for fastest iteration.

title: Prompt prefix KV caching for tinygrad backend
description:
tinygrad.apps.llm.Transformer.generate hardcodes start_pos=0 on entry (line 224 of llm.py), so the internal cache_kv tensor is overwritten from position 0 on every generate() call.  Prompts are fully re-encoded even when the new prompt shares a long prefix with the previous one -- agentic loops (egg-mono + multi-turn tool use) pay the full prefill cost every turn.

Fix sketch:
1. Subclass Transformer or monkey-patch generate() so start_pos is respected.
2. TinygradBackend remembers the prompt_tokens of the last request plus how many positions the KV cache currently represents.
3. On new generate, find longest common prefix (bounded by max_context), pass start_pos=prefix_len and tokens=full_prompt to the patched generate.  The patched code only encodes tokens[prefix_len:] as prefill and reads the cache for the prefix.

Risks:
- cache_kv is per-TransformerBlock; need to ensure ALL blocks see the same start_pos.
- If keep_packed=True, the prefill path currently materialises weights anyway; prefix caching's win would be on top of that.

Rough perf estimate: on Qwen3-8B with 1000-token prompts, saves ~60s/turn when only the last ~100 tokens change (typical tool-result append).  Meaningful for interactive use.

title: tinygrad backend: weights dequantized to fp16 at load, losing runtime memory savings
description: tinygrad.apps.llm.Transformer.from_gguf casts every weight tensor to float16 at load time (nn.state.ggml_data_to_tensor already dequantizes Q4_0/Q4_K/etc. to float32 during gguf_load). So a Q4_K_S GGUF that's 5 GB on disk occupies ~16 GB of VRAM for an 8B model at runtime -- same as an F16 file. This cancels the practical benefit of quantization on memory-constrained GPUs. Observed 2026-04-18 with Qwen3-8B Q4_K_S + --context-length 8192 using ~22 GB on a 24 GB card. Two remediation paths: (a) patch tinygrad upstream to keep weights in GGML layout and add a dequantize-on-matmul op (big change), or (b) prioritize the llama-cpp-python backend (workspace-04j) which keeps weights quantized natively. Meanwhile users must downshift to smaller models (e.g. Qwen3-4B) or tolerate the overhead.

title: Structured output (JSON mode): support response_format={type:json_schema}
description: OpenAI response_format=json_schema / json_object. Uses the same grammar/constraint machinery that tool calls need (GBNF + format-specific schema injection). On step backends: constrained sampling; on constraint backends: pass schema through. Natural extension of Phase 2 Phase 4 work — once grammar generation lands (workspace-2mu), structured output is a small additional surface.
close_reason: implemented: response_format plumbing end-to-end. openai.py parses response_format from body and forwards to Orchestrator.chat_completion (new response_format kwarg). Orchestrator dispatches: json_object -> generic JSON grammar via generate_json_mode_gbnf(None); json_schema -> schema-constrained grammar via generate_json_mode_gbnf(schema). Grammar attached to CompiledRequest.grammar (and json_schema field populated for structured_outputs backends). Without a grammar-aware backend the field is a hint; once llama-cpp-python (workspace-04j) lands, JSON mode enforcement is automatic. 6 new tests cover the grammar generation, CompiledRequest population, and API-level pass-through.

title: Tool-result injection: render tool outputs back into chat template per-format
description: Symmetry with tool-call parsing. When a client sends a message with role='tool' (or tool_results), the ChatTemplate.render() must insert that into the prompt using the format the model expects: Hermes <tool_response>, Mistral [TOOL_RESULTS], Llama 3 tool role messages, DeepSeek <｜tool▁output▁begin｜>, etc. Same format-registry machinery as outbound parsing, just the inverse direction. Required to close the full tool-calling loop (currently clients can get tool calls out, but feeding results back in relies on whatever Jinja the template does).
close_reason:
Fixed ChatTemplate._msg_to_dict double-encoding of tool_calls.function.arguments.  OpenAI spec carries arguments as a JSON string, but every chat template (Hermes, Qwen, Llama3, Mistral, DeepSeek) expects an object.  When rendered directly, the string would land as '"arguments": "{\"city\": \"Zagreb\"}"' -- malformed-looking from the model's perspective, which made the LLM ignore the prior tool call and loop back to re-calling the tool instead of using the result.

Fix: new _parse_tool_arguments helper tries json.loads() on the string; if it parses, pass the dict to the template; if not, pass the string through unchanged (preserves info on malformed bodies).  Role=tool messages with tool_call_id already flowed through correctly via _msg_to_dict -> template -- Qwen2.5's template wraps them in <tool_response> on a user turn, which is the format the model was trained on.

Verified end-to-end with Qwen2.5-0.5B GGUF: a 4-message conversation (user asks -> assistant tool_call -> tool result -> generation prompt) now renders a prompt where (a) prior tool call is 'arguments': {"city": "Zagreb"} (object), (b) tool result 'sunny, 22 C' appears wrapped in <tool_response>, (c) generation prompt is at the end.  9 new regression tests (parse helper, single/multi tool_result, parallel calls with results in order).  All 94 unit tests pass.

title: Investigate literal {{ in Hermes tool-call stream
description: Streaming tool-call delta includes literal {{ at start instead of single {. Possibly a chat-template rendering artifact (Jinja escaping) or model output quirk. Check omnitool/template.py rendering of tool prompt for Qwen2.5 and verify against reference rendering.
close_reason: Fixed via egg-toolbox/egg_toolbox/formats/hermes.py refactor: new _parse_tool_call_body helper does strict JSON -> double-brace repair -> trailing-comma repair -> regex fallback. _process_in_tool now buffers full JSON body instead of streaming raw token chunks (which polluted 'arguments' with 'name' field text). _commit_buffered_tool_call always advances _tool_index, so finish_reason=tool_calls fires even for malformed bodies that opened <tool_call>. Verified with Qwen2.5-0.5B + real curl: name='get_weather', arguments='{"city":"Zagreb"}', finish_reason='tool_calls' in both streaming and non-streaming. Verified end-to-end via egg-mono stream_chat() with tools param — received clean tool_calls object. 23/23 unit tests still pass. The {{ quirk (workspace-ome) is from Qwen's tokenizer occasionally merging { with preceding whitespace into a single BPE token that decodes as {{; parser handles it robustly now.

title: Phase 4: Production hardening
description: Error recovery, timeouts, parallel tool calls, token tracking, grammar-guided speculative decoding. See docs/omnitool-architecture.md Sections 14.5 and 15.

title: Phase 2: GBNF grammar generation
description: Implement grammar.py: JSON schema to GBNF conversion for Hermes format. See docs/omnitool-architecture.md Section 12.
close_reason: implemented egg_toolbox/grammar.py: generate_gbnf(tools, analysis) dispatches on tool_mode to _hermes_gbnf / _llama3_gbnf / _generic_json_gbnf. Core _json_schema_to_gbnf handles string, integer, number, boolean, null, enum, object-with-properties, array-with-items. Each Hermes tool gets its own rule coupling the name literal with its own args rule so the grammar can't mix tool-A name with tool-B schema. $ref/oneOf/anyOf/allOf explicitly raise NotImplementedError. 15 unit tests. HermesHandler.generate_grammar now returns the generated grammar; other format handlers unchanged (still return None until their own generate_grammar is wired). Grammar is ready for consumption by llama-cpp-python backend (workspace-04j) and future tinygrad grammar sampler. Unblocks workspace-jcg (JSON mode).

title: Phase 2: Anthropic API (/v1/messages)
description: Implement api/anthropic.py projecting SemanticEvents to Anthropic content_block_start/delta/stop SSE format. See docs/omnitool-architecture.md Section 11.
close_reason: Shipped egg_toolbox/api/anthropic.py + /v1/messages route in middleware.py. Reuses the SemanticEvent IR: _BlockProjector tracks Anthropic block lifecycle (text / thinking / tool_use with sequential indices), emitting content_block_start/delta/stop + message_start/delta/stop as named SSE events. Input parser handles top-level 'system' (string or blocks), tool_use echoes, and tool_result user blocks (expanded to role=tool ChatMessages). Deferred content_block_start for tool_use until TOOL_CALL_NAME arrives because Anthropic requires the name at block start and our IR provides it after START. 11 new tests covering: validation (missing messages/max_tokens/invalid-json), non-streaming text + tool_use + usage, streaming event sequence + text reconstruction + tool deltas (input_json_delta), and input shapes (system as string/blocks, tool_result round-trip). 83/83 unit tests pass.

title: Phase 2: All format handlers (llama3, mistral, deepseek, functionary, command_r, harmony, generic)
description: Implement per-format state machine parsers for all remaining tool calling formats. Each has its own state machine, FormatHandler, and FormatParserState. See docs/omnitool-architecture.md Section 7 for handler details and Section 8.1 for the Harmony multi-channel handler.
close_reason: All 7 format handlers shipped across commits 1789ad2, 24f7cf5, 3fb2189, 1fdd8ef, ce6481a: Llama 3 (<|python_tag|> + JSON and Python-call body), Mistral ([TOOL_CALLS] JSON array, bracket-aware scanner), DeepSeek (fullwidth ｜/▁ markers + fenced JSON), Command-R (<|START_ACTION|> JSON array with tool_name/parameters), Functionary v3 (<function=name>) and v3.1 (>>>name with bracket-counted body), Harmony (multi-channel: analysis/commentary/final; accepts both simplified and full gpt-oss protocol), Generic (leading-JSON heuristic fallback, rejects JSON-in-prose). 72/72 tests pass. All handlers follow the buffer-then-emit discipline so raw JSON chunks never pollute tool_arguments, and always advance _tool_index so finish_reason=tool_calls fires even on malformed bodies.

title: Phase 2: Full format detector (differential template analysis)
description: Port llama.cpp autoparser's differential template analysis algorithm. Render template with sentinel values, diff outputs, extract structural markers, classify format. Should correctly detect format for 20+ popular model templates. See docs/omnitool-architecture.md Section 6 and 14.4.

title: Packed-quant kernels: Q5_0, Q2_K, IQ-quants
description: Cover long-tail quant types. Q5_0 is a common miss today (blocks some community quants). Q2_K for ultra-low-bit 70B+ models. IQ-quants (IQ4_XS, IQ3_M) are increasingly popular for small models. Lower priority than the common four but needed for full parity.

title: Analyze request batching with tinygrad backend
description: Investigate whether and how we can batch concurrent generate_tokens requests on the tinygrad backend (batch dim B > 1 in Transformer.forward), allowing 2+ chat completions to share a forward pass. Questions: (a) does tinygrad's cache_kv layout support B>1 per-request KV? (b) how to handle mixed prompt lengths / mixed generation lengths without wasting compute on finished rows? (c) interplay with the single-thread sqlite kernel cache pin. (d) interplay with prefix caching -- can two requests with different shared prefixes batch? (e) throughput vs latency tradeoff on 4090 for Qwen3-8B. Output: a design note and either a prototype branch or an explicit 'no, not worth it' recommendation with numbers.

title: tinygrad JITBEAM deadlocks with --keep-packed + multiprocessing beam pool
description:
Repro: run egg-toolbox with --keep-packed and JITBEAM>=2 env var, then fire a curl at /v1/chat/completions.  Server hangs at ~10 GB GPU memory, 0% GPU util, indefinitely.

Observed 2026-04-18 with Qwen3-8B-Q4_0, ctx=2048, tinygrad installed from pip (has Q4_0/Q4_K/Q6_K/MXFP4 support but not Q1_0).

Diagnostics:
- Main process in futex_wait_queue state
- ~20-33 multiprocessing spawn workers all sleeping in futex_wait_queue
- ~/.cache/tinygrad/cache.db grows to 138 MB with active -wal file
- Initial beam search progresses fine (first 70s produces ~1500 log lines completing one kernel at 41/41), then on subsequent beam search start GPU jumps from 5.5 GB to 10 GB and all workers go silent

Likely cause: sqlite WAL lock contention on cache.db between main process (reader for cache hits) and worker processes (writer for new beam results).  The lock remains held somehow across a beam search handoff.

Workaround: run with JITBEAM=0 (no beam).  Decode works but slow (~0.07-0.17 tok/s on 4090 for Qwen3-8B-Q4_0 with --keep-packed).

Upstream fix likely required (tinygrad engine/jit.py or codegen/opt/search.py beam_pool).  Filed workspace-30s tracks the broader 'dequant-on-load' limitation that drives the need for --keep-packed.

title: Phase 3: Backend expansion (llamacpp, vllm, sglang)
description: Implement backends/llamacpp.py, backends/vllm.py, backends/sglang.py. See docs/omnitool-architecture.md Section 9.
notes: Deprioritized 2026-04-18. Scope reframed: egg-toolbox is a tinygrad-native runtime layer (see epic workspace-mu5), not a universal frontend. vLLM and SGLang already ship their own OpenAI-compatible tool-calling servers; competing is not the project goal. llama-cpp-python remains a potential escape hatch if a model needs native quantized weights or long-context features tinygrad cannot yet match, but the main thrust is to bring tinygrad itself to llama.cpp-level capability.

value: Prefix KV caching in egg-toolbox tinygrad backend is single-tenant by design (2026-04-18): it tracks self._cache_tokens = last-request's prompt+output and reuses cache_kv only when the NEXT request shares a token prefix. Works for sequential chat in one conversation; cross-conversation requests evict each other's cache (divergence at pos 0 -> full prefill). Multi-tenant/radix-tree prefix caching (vLLM/SGLang style, many concurrent conversations each keeping their cache) is intentionally deferred -- different design (keyed cache, eviction policy, much larger GPU memory), different issue. If that becomes a need, the token-prefix invariant (self._cache_tokens matches cache_kv[0..N) exactly) generalizes cleanly to a (session_id -> cache_tokens) map plus per-session cache_kv slices.

Project renamed: omnitool -> egg-toolbox (2026-04-18). Python module: egg_toolbox (underscore). PyPI/CLI: egg-toolbox (hyphen). Project dir: /home/albert/Private/Projekti/ai/omnitool/egg-toolbox/ (repo root /omnitool/ kept for now). egg-mono provider entry at egg-mono/eggconfig/eggconfig/data/models.json uses provider key 'egg-toolbox' with api_base http://127.0.0.1:8765/v1/chat/completions and EGG_TOOLBOX_API_KEY env var.

HermesParser design rule: buffer the entire <tool_call>...</tool_call> body before emitting TOOL_ARGS_DELTA. Streaming raw token chunks inside the tool-call body pollutes the OpenAI 'arguments' projection with the 'name' field text and JSON structural noise. Instead: accumulate _json_buffer, emit NAME+ARGS_DELTA(full)+COMMIT on close. Always advance _tool_index so finish_reason=tool_calls fires even if JSON parsing fails — the model clearly intended a tool call by opening <tool_call>. See _parse_tool_call_body in egg_toolbox/formats/hermes.py for JSON-repair fallbacks (double-brace, trailing commas, regex).

tinygrad's Transformer ignores attn_{q,k,v}.bias tensors (nn.Linear bias=False), which breaks Qwen2/Qwen2.5 — silent garbage output. Fix in omnitool/backends/tinygrad.py via _from_gguf_with_qkv_bias helper that attaches zero-init bias Tensors to each block before load_state_dict. 72 bias keys for Qwen2.5-0.5B, without fix model produces random script-switching tokens.

Orchestrator/TinygradBackend threading architecture (2026-04-18): (1) TinygradBackend owns a ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) so model load + every generate_tokens runs on the same OS thread (tinygrad's sqlite kernel cache is thread-bound). (2) Orchestrator owns a separate ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) for the async->sync bridge; producer reads backend.generate_tokens into an asyncio.Queue via call_soon_threadsafe so the event loop stays responsive per-token. (3) Cancellation uses an explicit backend.cancel_generation() method that sets a threading.Event; orchestrator's finally calls it. Do NOT use generator.close() for this -- that's cross-thread on an iterating generator and raises 'generator already executing' which is easy to silently swallow. (4) Queue between backend's _run and generate_tokens's consumer is UNBOUNDED so _run never blocks on put() before it can check the cancel flag.

Session 2026-04-18 summary: Phase 2 format handlers ALL shipped (Hermes, Llama3, Mistral, DeepSeek, Command-R, Functionary v3/v3.1, Harmony, Generic). Anthropic API /v1/messages shipped. Streaming fixed (thread-bridge, cancellation). Tool-result injection fixed (_parse_tool_arguments, always-emit-content-key). tinygrad backend: Qwen2 bias fix, explicit cancellation, single-thread pinning, keep_packed option, context-length flag, disable-thinking flag (opt-in only). 100/100 tests pass. Remaining tinygrad issues: workspace-30s (dequant-on-load wastes VRAM), workspace-2gi (JITBEAM+keep-packed deadlock), workspace-ok0 (no prompt prefix cache). Next Phase 2 work ready: workspace-2mu (GBNF grammar), workspace-o4g (full detector), workspace-jcg (JSON mode), workspace-04j (llama-cpp-python backend).
